channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Hezbollah steps up rocket fire as Israel sends more troops into Lebanon
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Hezbollah fired another barrage of rockets into Israel on Tuesday, and the militant group’s acting leader vowed to keep up pressure that has forced tens of thousands of Israelis from their homes near the Lebanese border.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE and TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Survivors of massacre in small Haitian town where 70 died point finger at government
item.description: PONT-SONDÉ, Haiti (AP) — Angry whispers have broken the heavy silence that fell over Pont-Sondé just days after a vicious gang attack left more than 70 dead, marking one of Haiti’s biggest massacres in recent history.
item.dc:creator: PIERRE-RICHARD LUXAMA and DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Musk's X to be reinstated in Brazil after complying with Supreme Court demands
item.description: The Brazilian Supreme Court’s Justice Alexandre de Moraes on Tuesday authorized the restoration of social media platform X´s service in Brazil, over a month after its nationwide shutdown, according to a statement posted on the court’s website.
item.dc:creator: Gabriela Sá Pessoa And Barbara Ortutay, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Colombian electoral authorities open investigation against President Petro's 2022 campaign
item.description: Electoral authorities in Colombia have ruled in favor of investigating financial misconduct allegations against the 2022 campaign that got President Gustavo Petro elected.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Israel defense minister cancels visit with Pentagon chief
item.description: Israel has canceled a planned Wednesday meeting between its Defense Minister Yoav Gallant and U.S. Defense Secretary Lloyd Austin, the Pentagon confirmed Tuesday. “We were just informed that Minister Gallant will be postponing his trip to Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: Hungary’s Orbán interrupted by activist during EU presidency news conference
item.description: BUDAPEST, Hungary (AP) — An activist and municipal councilor for a Hungarian opposition party disrupted a news conference in the French city of Strasbourg on Tuesday, as Prime Minister Viktor Orbán was laying out his plans for Hungary's six-month presidency of the European Union.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Korea’s Kim Jong Un pushes goal to become nuclear powerhouse in warning to South Korea
item.description: Kim Jong Un issued his second ominous message, saying he plans to make Pyongyang a top nuclear player and expand its capabilities in face of South Korea-U.S. partnership.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former Taiwan leader Tsai to visit Czech Republic, other European destinations, drawing China's ire
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — Former Taiwan leader Tsai Ing-wen is to visit Czech Republic, and other European destinations, drawing China’s ire.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Water gushes through sand dunes after a rare rainfall in the Sahara desert
item.description: A rare deluge of rainfall has left lagoons of water amid the palm trees and sand dunes of the Sahara desert.
item.dc:creator: Sam Metz And Baba Ahmed, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: No evidence of major fuel spill on Samoan reef where New Zealand navy ship sank
item.description: WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — Divers and marine experts found no evidence of a major fuel spill on a Samoan reef Tuesday after a New Zealand navy ship ran aground and sank, Samoa's deputy prime minister said.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russian court sentences a 72-year-old American to nearly 7 years in prison for fighting in Ukraine
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — A Russian court on Monday sentenced a 72-year-old American in a closed trial to nearly seven years in prison for allegedly fighting as a mercenary in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Middle East latest: Palestinian militants in Gaza fire rockets into Israel as it marks Oct. 7
item.description: Palestinian militants in Gaza fired a barrage of rockets into Israel on Monday as mourners marked the anniversary of the Oct. 7 attack, without disrupting a nearby ceremony.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: War rages on multiple fronts as Israel marks a year since Hamas' Oct. 7 attack
item.description: RE'IM, Israel (AP) — Israelis held somber ceremonies Monday to mark a year since the deadliest attack in the country’s history, a Hamas-led raid that shattered its sense of security and has since spiraled into wars on two fronts with no end in sight. Hamas marked the anniversary of its Oct.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN and TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Tunisia's Kais Saied wins landslide reelection, entrenching his power in Arab Spring's birthplace
item.description: TUNIS, Tunisia (AP) — President Kais Saied won a landslide victory in Tunisia's election Monday, keeping his grip on power after a first term in which opponents were imprisoned and the country's institutions overhauled to give him more authority.
item.dc:creator: BOUAZZA BEN BOUAZZA and SAM METZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Reporter's Notebook: Iran’s missiles strike Israel, and a deadly terror attack in Tel Aviv
item.description: On the same day Iran attacked Israel with some 181 missiles last week, a terrorist attack saw 7 innocent Israelis murdered in cold blood by two Palestinians in the Tel Aviv area.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kenya’s deputy president defends himself before impeachment
item.description: NAIROBI, Kenya (AP) — Kenya’s deputy president, facing an impeachment motion in which he's accused of supporting anti-government protests in June and of being involved in corruption and other irregularities, has defended himself and confirmed that he would appear before parliament on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: EVELYNE MUSAMBI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Watch: Harris, Emhoff mark anniversary of Oct. 7 attacks
item.description: Vice President Harris and second gentleman Doug Emhoff on Monday marked the first anniversary of the Oct. 7, 2023, attacks by Hamas on Israel. The two were expected to plant a memorial tree at the vice president’s residence, in addition to delivering remarks to the press.
item.dc:creator: The Hill

item.title: Trump: Israel has to 'get smart' about supporting him
item.description: Former President Trump said Monday Israel has to “get smart” about supporting him and suggested Jewish people have not adequately backed him after he took several pro-Israel actions while in office.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Russia jails American Stephen Hubbard over fighting as a mercenary in Ukraine
item.description: Stephen Hubbard, 72, of Michigan, has been sentenced by a Russian court Monday after being convicted of fighting as a mercenary in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russian arms dealer Viktor Bout, who was traded for Brittney Griner, to sell weapons to Iran-backed Houthis
item.description: Russian "Merchant of Death" Viktor Bout has reportedly resumed his role as an arms dealer following his December 2022 release from U.S. custody.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: American father of Hamas hostage Itay Chen pushes US, Israel on ‘Plan B’ as negotiations falter
item.description: The father of an American held by Hamas is urging the Biden administration to think outside of the box and return his son and others from Gaza as Israel's year-long strategy fails to free them.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Abigail Edan's aunt says 'we need a deal' on Oct. 7 anniversary
item.description: "I say this to the Palestinian people, and I say this to the Israelis Diplomacy now is what we need," Naftali said.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: New film documents Oct. 7 attacks after first anniversary
item.description: Pierre Rehov, speaking from Ashdod, Israel, said he was disturbed by reactions that seemed to excuse the attack.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Israel-Hamas war will affect generations: Humanitarian worker
item.description: Since Israel's offensive began after the October 7 attacks, the death toll in Gaza is estimated at 41,800, according to the Palestinian Health Authority.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Netanyahu calls Macron, other Western leaders who support arms embargo against Israel a ‘disgrace’
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu slammed Western leaders on Saturday who said they support an arms embargo on Israel over how its handling the Gaza war.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Expert warns UN's role in AI regulation could lead to safety overreach
item.description: Nations have pursued a number of options to better globally coordinate artificial intelligence safety initiatives, including several safety summits and the creation of safety institutes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexico’s new president promises to resume fight against climate change
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — In her first days as Mexico’s new president, Claudia Sheinbaum made a point of distancing herself from the fossil fuel reliance promoted by her predecessor and mentor, Andrés Manuel López Obrador, and vowed to resume an energy transition that he halted.
item.dc:creator: TERESA DE MIGUEL, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Middle East latest: An Israeli airstrike cuts a major highway linking Lebanon with Syria
item.description: An Israeli airstrike has cut off a main highway linking Lebanon with Syria, leaving two huge craters on either side of the road.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: North Korea's Kim threatens to destroy South Korea with nuclear strikes if provoked
item.description: The latest comments come during heightened animosities over the North’s recent disclosure of a nuclear facility.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Two predawn explosions rock the area around the Israeli Embassy in Copenhagen but no one is injured
item.description: COPENHAGEN, Denmark (AP) — Three young Swedes were arrested Wednesday in connection with two predawn explosions that occurred in the vicinity of the Israeli Embassy in Copenhagen, prompting a nearby Jewish school to close for the day. Police said no one was injured.
item.dc:creator: JAN M. OLSEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: A US bomb from World War II explodes at a Japanese airport, causing a large crater in a taxiway
item.description: Officials said an investigation by the Self-Defense Forces and police confirmed that the explosion was caused by a 500-pound U.S. bomb and there was no further danger.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel, Iran swap threats as strikes on Lebanon continue
item.description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has vowed to retaliate, saying Iran “made a big mistake" by launching a missile attack.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation
